scope: Next 20 incomplete transfer rows after transfer-research-003, sorted by source row
method: Research player role/minutes expectation from roster pages, school announcements, transfer reports, projected lineups, and team context.
Use historical rating from the imported spreadsheet as the historical anchor.
Translate evidence into the manual input fields used by the spreadsheet: starter, role, opportunity change, offensive burden, NBA projection, and upside/tools.
Cap starter/role/opportunity when projected-lineup or depth-chart evidence is unclear.
Use larger batches only when each player still has individualized research notes and evidence.

teamContextRubric.projectedStarter: Use Yes only with projected-lineup evidence, explicit likely-starter reporting, or a roster context with a clear open starting path. Use Uncertain for high-minute players without clear starting evidence.
teamContextRubric.projectedRole: Cap likely rotation or specialist profiles in the 60-75 range. Use 80-89 for likely starters or top-four options. Use 90+ only for projected primary/near-primary stars.
teamContextRubric.opportunityChange: Do not reward prior production by itself. Lower-major stars moving into crowded high-major rosters should usually sit in the 45-65 range unless reporting shows a clear role increase.
teamContextRubric.offensiveBurden: Tie burden to expected usage within the new lineup, not just prior usage.
teamContextRubric.roleCap: Each researched player gets a role cap based on projected lineup, returning stars, incoming stars, position competition, and starter evidence.

players.researchSummary: Beasley has a strong path to major minutes because Washington specifically targeted his speed, paint pressure and scoring. The Huskies' own announcement says he was one of their top point guards on the board and that they needed his scoring in the Big Ten. I kept starter at Uncertain because I did not find a projected lineup, but the role/opportunity profile is clearly above ordinary rotation level.

teamContext.projectedLineup: No direct projected starting lineup found.
teamContext.returningStars: Washington is adding Beasley to stabilize guard creation after a transfer-heavy offseason.
teamContext.incomingStars: No single higher-profile incoming point guard was found in this research pass.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Big Ten jump creates translation risk, but he fills a clear creation need.
teamContext.starterEvidence: strong school fit language but no starting-five confirmation
teamContext.roleCap: likely high-minute guard; cap low-80s until lineup evidence confirms starter status

evidence.notes: Confirms signing; coach calls him one of the top point guards on Washington's board and highlights scoring/creation need.
Notes Second Team All-WCC season with 13.6 ppg and 4.0 apg.

players.researchSummary: Burks started every game for an NCAA Tournament UCF team and gives Oklahoma State size, shooting and versatility. Oklahoma State also added a large portal class and several freshmen, so I like him as a starting-caliber or top-six forward but not enough to call him a definite starter without lineup evidence.

teamContext.projectedLineup: No direct projected starting lineup found.
teamContext.returningStars: Oklahoma State returns some pieces but heavily rebuilt through the portal.
teamContext.incomingStars: Luka Bogavac, Kashie Natt, Julius Halaifonua, Andrija Grbovic and freshmen add role competition.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Burks' shooting and 6-9 size help him stand out, but the roster has many new forward options.
teamContext.starterEvidence: strong production and school fit language, not direct lineup proof
teamContext.roleCap: starting-caliber forward/top-six piece; cap high-70s due roster crowding

evidence.notes: Confirms Burks signing; notes 33 starts, 13.3 ppg, 4.8 rpg and 37.3 percent from three.
Frames Burks as the sixth portal addition and lists Oklahoma State roster additions.

players.researchSummary: Riley is a veteran offensive creator joining a Cincinnati roster that was reset under Jerrod Calhoun. His Tulsa season was productive, but Cincinnati has Myles Colvin, Elijah Perryman, Grant Darbyshire and other guards, and the efficiency concerns noted by local coverage make me stop short of a huge score.

teamContext.projectedLineup: No direct projected starting lineup found.
teamContext.returningStars: Cincinnati's roster is rebuilt, but Myles Colvin and other guards remain in the mix.
teamContext.incomingStars: Tyler Tejada and Riley Allenspach join as major frontcourt additions, while Riley fills veteran guard creation.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Guard competition is real, but Riley is the most proven veteran creator among the new guards.
teamContext.starterEvidence: production and roster-need fit, no direct starting-five evidence
teamContext.roleCap: likely top-six guard; cap around 80 because efficiency/Big 12 translation risk remains

evidence.notes: Confirms Cincinnati roster placement and Tulsa production: 15.0 ppg, 4.4 apg, All-American Conference honors.
Profiles Riley as a scorer while raising efficiency/Big 12 translation questions.
Shows 2026-27 Cincinnati roster and guard/frontcourt competition.

players.researchSummary: Kennedy has one of the best opportunity profiles in this batch. Wake Forest is in a near-total rebuild, and recent ACC preview coverage names him as a key player. He was both MAAC Player and Rookie of the Year as a freshman, so the development/upside path is real. I left starter as Uncertain only because no projected lineup was found.

teamContext.projectedLineup: No formal projected lineup found, but Wake preview coverage highlights Kennedy as a key player.
teamContext.returningStars: Wake has only a small returning core after significant roster turnover.
teamContext.incomingStars: Jamari McDowell and several frontcourt/wing transfers join, but Kennedy is the most proven creator.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Justin Ray and other guards create some competition, but Kennedy's production/age combination stands out.
teamContext.starterEvidence: major-role team-context evidence, not direct lineup proof
teamContext.roleCap: likely high-minute creator; cap mid-80s until starter reporting appears

evidence.notes: Wake preview names Kennedy among key players in a near-total rebuild.
Confirms MAAC Player and Rookie of the Year honors.
Lists 18.4 ppg, 4.6 rpg and 4.2 apg as a freshman.

players.researchSummary: Nwoko has direct starter evidence from the CBS Big East projected lineup and a clean role fit as Xavier's physical center. He started 29 games at LSU and Pitino expects immediate impact. Opportunity is high, but offensive burden stays below Westry/Milicevic levels because Xavier has multiple creators and frontcourt scorers.

teamContext.projectedLineup: CBS projects Nwoko as Xavier's starting center.
teamContext.returningStars: Jovan Milicevic and Gabriel Pozzato are important returning pieces.
teamContext.incomingStars: Chance Westry, Tru Washington and Ruben Dominguez create a stronger perimeter group.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Nikos Chitikoudis and Kason Westphal add center depth but Nwoko is the projected starter.
teamContext.starterEvidence: direct projected-lineup evidence and school immediate-impact language
teamContext.roleCap: projected starting center; cap mid-80s because Xavier's offense is perimeter-catalyst driven

evidence.notes: Confirms signing; Nwoko started 29 games at LSU and averaged 13.4 ppg and 5.9 rpg.
Projects Xavier starting lineup with Nwoko at center.
Shows 2026-27 Xavier roster context.

players.researchSummary: Faulkner was a highly productive SoCon big with first-team and all-defense honors, so his historical anchor stays strong. The role is capped because Clemson added multiple transfers and has frontcourt competition, and Faulkner's non-shooting profile could make usage more matchup-dependent at the ACC level.

teamContext.projectedLineup: No direct projected starting lineup found.
teamContext.returningStars: Clemson has returning size and added several transfers.
teamContext.incomingStars: Cole Certa, Liutauras Lelevicius and David Fuchs join the transfer class with Faulkner.
teamContext.positionCompetition: David Fuchs and returning forwards create frontcourt role competition.
teamContext.starterEvidence: strong production and roster fit, no direct lineup proof
teamContext.roleCap: high-minutes frontcourt option; cap high-70s because ACC translation and frontcourt fit are uncertain

evidence.notes: Confirms Clemson transfer class including Faulkner.
Lists 18.0 ppg, 9.5 rpg, 2.0 apg and 1.5 bpg with All-SoCon First Team/All-Defensive honors.
Confirms production and efficiency profile.

players.researchSummary: Weeks is a proven scorer moving into a Memphis roster that needed transfer help after a down season. His Southern Miss usage was huge and Memphis coverage frames him as a major addition. Starter is still Uncertain because the Tigers have a large transfer class and I did not find a reliable projected lineup.

teamContext.projectedLineup: No direct projected starting lineup found.
teamContext.returningStars: Memphis had limited continuity after a poor 2025-26 season.
teamContext.incomingStars: Memphis has a large portal class, including Maxim Logue, Corneilous Williams and multiple guards/wings.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Weeks should be one of the better scorers but not necessarily the only frontcourt/wing option.
teamContext.starterEvidence: production and roster-need evidence, no direct lineup proof
teamContext.roleCap: likely top-six scoring forward; cap low-80s due roster uncertainty

evidence.notes: Reports Weeks committed to Memphis and ranked top 40 nationally in scoring at 19.7 ppg.
Details his Southern Miss scoring and JUCO background.
Confirms Memphis commitment.

players.researchSummary: Certa's late-season Notre Dame scoring spike is real, and Clemson specifically added him as an ACC-tested guard. The lower historical anchor keeps the BBPR modest, while the projection inputs reflect a plausible rotation scorer with upside but no confirmed starting role.

teamContext.projectedLineup: No direct projected starting lineup found.
teamContext.returningStars: Clemson has returning guards and wings plus a full transfer class.
teamContext.incomingStars: Dylan Faulkner, Liutauras Lelevicius and David Fuchs also join.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Certa is a proven ACC scorer but may compete for a sixth-man or secondary guard role.
teamContext.starterEvidence: ACC starts and late scoring surge, not direct lineup proof
teamContext.roleCap: rotation scorer with starter upside; cap mid-70s until lineup evidence appears

evidence.notes: Confirms Certa signing; notes 16 starts at Notre Dame, 12.8 ppg and 19.5 ppg over final 11 games.
Shows Clemson 2026-27 roster context.

players.researchSummary: Meadow is a proven Mountain West starter and efficient shooter, but Oregon's roster is extremely crowded with transfers and high-end young talent. I see him as a useful rotation forward/shooter, but not a clear starter or major offensive burden player without lineup evidence.

teamContext.projectedLineup: No direct projected starting lineup found.
teamContext.returningStars: Oregon returns Sean Stewart and has several high-upside young players.
teamContext.incomingStars: Tajh Ariza, Jasper Johnson, Jerry Easter II, Taylor Bol Bowen, Pharaoh Compton and others create heavy competition.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Forward/wing depth is crowded, limiting role certainty.
teamContext.starterEvidence: Oregon signing/roster confirmation and Boise production, no lineup proof
teamContext.roleCap: rotation forward/shooter; cap mid-70s due crowded Oregon roster

evidence.notes: Confirms signing; Meadow started all 32 games at Boise State and averaged 12.1 ppg on 38.6 percent from three.
Shows Oregon's large transfer class and roster reset.
Shows 2026-27 roster depth at wing/forward.

players.researchSummary: Dowd is a winning, productive transfer with rebounding and multi-position defense that Auburn clearly values. Auburn's frontcourt is packed with Owen Freeman, Bukky Oboye, Adam Olsen and others, so the opportunity is capped despite the double-double track record.

teamContext.projectedLineup: No direct projected starting lineup found.
teamContext.returningStars: Tahaad Pettiford and other guards shape the offensive hierarchy.
teamContext.incomingStars: Owen Freeman, Bukky Oboye, Adam Olsen, Narcisse Ngoy and Caleb Williams create a deep group.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Dowd's versatility helps, but Auburn has several frontcourt options.
teamContext.starterEvidence: strong fit comments and production, no direct lineup proof
teamContext.roleCap: high-minute versatile forward; cap high-70s due Auburn depth

evidence.notes: Confirms signing; notes 14.5 ppg, 10.1 rpg, First Team All-Sun Belt and coach fit comments.
Shows 2026-27 Auburn roster and frontcourt competition.
Summer Q&A frames Dowd as trying to prove his production at the high-major level.

players.researchSummary: Holcombe has direct starter evidence from Utah On SI and was UVU's top two-way player. Utah needed a roster rebuild and his size/defense/playmaking fit a major role. The score is high, but not 90+, because shooting limitations and Big 12 translation create some cap.

teamContext.projectedLineup: Utah On SI projects Holcombe as the starting small forward.
teamContext.returningStars: Utah has significant roster turnover and a new-ish build under Alex Jensen.
teamContext.incomingStars: Styles Clemmons, Zati Loubaki, Alec Anigbata and others compete for roles.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Alec Anigbata and Jaxon Johnson provide forward competition, but Holcombe is projected ahead.
teamContext.starterEvidence: direct projected-lineup evidence plus first-team WAC/all-defense production
teamContext.roleCap: projected starter/major two-way wing; cap high-80s due shooting limitation

evidence.notes: Confirms signing; notes Lou Henson finalist, First-Team All-WAC and WAC All-Defensive honors.
Lists 16.0 ppg, 7.0 rpg and 2.2 spg at Utah Valley.
Projects Holcombe as Utah's starting small forward.

players.researchSummary: Allenspach is a productive, efficient frontcourt connector, but Cincinnati's roster has multiple bigs and forward options including Tyler Tejada, Jayden Hastings and Deshaun Vaden. I see a real rotation path and possible starter path, but not enough evidence for a high opportunity jump.

teamContext.projectedLineup: No direct projected starting lineup found.
teamContext.returningStars: Cincinnati has several frontcourt bodies and a new system under Jerrod Calhoun.
teamContext.incomingStars: Tyler Tejada and Allenspach are major frontcourt additions.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Tejada, Hastings, Vaden and other forwards/bigs limit a clear offensive burden.
teamContext.starterEvidence: productive signing and coach fit quote, no lineup proof
teamContext.roleCap: rotation/starter-contender big; cap mid-70s due frontcourt competition

evidence.notes: Confirms roster placement; lists All-A-10 Second Team, 13.6 ppg and 6.1 rpg.
Reports signing and Calhoun's comments about his offensive connectivity and inside-out skill.
Shows Cincinnati frontcourt competition.

players.researchSummary: This is a mid-major role-up case. Staveskie has multiple seasons of high-usage guard production, returns to Ohio, and Akron's transfer tracker shows him as a major incoming guard. The historical BBPR stays modest, but role/opportunity should be high in the MAC.

teamContext.projectedLineup: No formal projected lineup found, but Akron roster context and profile point to a likely major guard role.
teamContext.returningStars: Akron lost several guards and added multiple transfers.
teamContext.incomingStars: Max Burton, Exavier Wilson, Colin White and Ty Pence join, but Staveskie is the proven scoring/creation guard.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Other transfer guards compete, but his production across UIW/Cleveland State/Canisius is the best proven creation profile.
teamContext.starterEvidence: role/path inference from roster rebuild and production
teamContext.roleCap: likely starting/lead guard at MAC level; allow high-80s role despite no published lineup

evidence.notes: Lists 17.3 ppg and 4.1 apg at UIW, plus prior Cleveland State production.
Reports Staveskie committed to Akron and should be a solid roster addition.
Akron transfer tracker lists Staveskie among incoming transfers after multiple departures.

players.researchSummary: Taylor is one of the better talent-upside bets in this batch. Florida State describes him as a high-upside creator, he was a four-star portal player, and he was invited to the Jayson Tatum Elite Camp. FSU has other wings and transfers, so starter remains Uncertain, but he should be a major piece.

teamContext.incomingStars: Shon Abaev, Sebastian Rancik, Anthony Robinson II and freshmen add competition.
teamContext.positionCompetition: FSU wing/forward competition is real, but Taylor's creation and athleticism stand out.

evidence.notes: Confirms signing; coach highlights creation, athleticism and upside.

Reports invitation to Jayson Tatum Elite Camp.

players.researchSummary: D'Augustino is a clear MAC role/opportunity case despite a low historical anchor. Ohio's announcement frames him as a homecoming scorer, and he led IU Indy in scoring/minutes with efficient splits. The starter call is an inference from production and level fit rather than a published lineup.

players.researchSummary: Diop is a high-upside Gonzaga frontcourt projection. Gonzaga signed him after a strong freshman season at Arizona State, On3 rates him extremely highly, and current Gonzaga coverage treats him as a key frontcourt piece next to Braden Huff. His NBA/upside score is the highest in the batch; offensive burden is capped because Huff is still the frontcourt headliner.

teamContext.projectedLineup: No formal starting lineup found, but current Gonzaga coverage frames Diop as a key frontcourt piece.
teamContext.returningStars: Braden Huff is the main frontcourt star and senior anchor.
teamContext.incomingStars: Nathan De Sousa and other pieces join, while guard uncertainty could shift offensive structure.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Huff and Izan Almansa affect role, but Diop's rim protection/upside should force major minutes.
teamContext.starterEvidence: key-player frontcourt context plus elite transfer profile
teamContext.roleCap: likely starter or near-starter big; cap high-80s because Huff carries major offensive gravity

evidence.notes: Confirms signing; lists 13.6 ppg, 5.8 rpg and 2.1 bpg as a freshman at Arizona State.
Frames Diop as a key Gonzaga frontcourt player entering 2026-27.
Lists Diop with a very high portal rating.
Notes Diop entered the portal after averaging 13.6 ppg, 5.8 rpg and setting ASU freshman block records.
